question_text: Which of the following is the correct definition of the Solar System?

options.label: A
options.text: The Sun and all the objects that orbit around it, including planets, moons, and asteroids

options.label: B
options.text: A group of stars that are held together by gravity

options.label: C
options.text: The region of space surrounding Earth

options.label: D
options.text: All the planets in the Universe

correct_answer: The Sun and all the objects that orbit around it, including planets, moons, and asteroids
explanation: The Solar System is made up of the Sun at the centre and everything that orbits around it because of the Sun's gravitational pull. This includes the eight planets, their moons, asteroids, and comets. The other options describe different things: option B describes a galaxy, option C is too vague, and option D is incorrect because not all planets in the Universe are in our Solar System.
